body.title: Specific-model request outside testing drops a system_alarm (caller punch-list)
body.intention: I intend that every fragile specific-model request surfaces as a deduped alarm naming the caller, so the stragglers get fixed.
body.description:
**Problem:** specific-model requests are fragile and must be driven to zero, but only the known ones are converted in T-callers-request-tiers. The rest are invisible until they break.

**Shape:** When a request names a specific model OUTSIDE a testing context, drop a system_alarm signature=`specific-model:<model>` with the caller in the breakdown. As callers are fixed, their entries go quiet and drop off; the alarm self-clears when the breakdown empties. Testing exception = an explicit request-level flag (e.g. `req.allow_explicit_model=True`) set ONLY by the proxy self-test and pytest fixtures — NEVER an inherited env var (so the suite doesn't spam and prod can't dodge).

**Affected files:** devices/inference/device.py (detect named-model + drop alarm), InferenceRequest (the testing flag), self_test_sources.py / test fixtures (set the flag).
**Design rules:** depends on T-system-alarms-primitive; testing flag is request-scoped, not env.
**Scope boundary:** IN — alarm-on-named-model + the request-scoped testing flag. OUT — the alarm primitive; converting callers (T-callers-request-tiers). Also needs T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier to be in place.
**Test plan:** a non-test caller naming a model produces a system_alarm `specific-model:<model>` with that caller in the breakdown; a request with `allow_explicit_model=True` (test fixture) produces NO alarm; the flag cannot be set via env var.
**Completion criteria:** named-model-from-non-test-caller -> deduped alarm with caller breakdown; test-flagged request -> no alarm; flag is request-scoped only (grep: not read from os.environ).

body.result: SUBSUMED (superseded, stricter) by T-inference-pin-gate-enforce (commit 1bfbc843). This ticket wanted: a specific-model request outside testing drops system_alarm signature='specific-model:<model>' with the caller, self-clearing as callers are fixed, gated by a testing flag. The pin-gate does MORE: an unsanctioned model pin is REJECTED (raise ValueError) AND raises system_alarm signature='unsanctioned-model-pin:<model>' with the caller (device.py:313-314), gated by SANCTIONED_PIN_REASONS (inference_test/akien_experiment/model_competition) — a stricter, sanctioned-reason gate rather than a single allow_explicit_model flag. Intent (drive specific-model to zero, alarm the rest) fully served.
body.unproven_reason: Superseded-close; the pin-gate's behavior is proven by test_pin_gate.py (unsanctioned pin raises+alarms), shipped under 1bfbc843.
